SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.io.vsam

Class EntrySequentialFile

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.io.AbstractFile
com.nib.commons.io.vsam.AbstractVsamFile
com.nib.commons.io.vsam.AbstractEntrySequentialFile
com.nib.commons.io.vsam.EntrySequentialFile
public class EntrySequentialFile extends AbstractEntrySequentialFile
Represents a sequential file for managing file I/O operations within a specific context or database connection. This class supports various operations such as reading, writing, and managing records in a sequential manner. It builds on the functionality provided by the AbstractEntrySequentialFile class.

The EntrySequentialFile class is designed to work with both database connections and context-based configurations. It provides an implementation of methods to handle file operations with error handling and status management.

  • Constructor Details

    • EntrySequentialFile

      public EntrySequentialFile(DatabaseConnection databaseConnection, FileDescriptor fileDescriptor)
      Constructs an EntrySequentialFile object with the specified DatabaseConnection and FileDescriptor. Initializes the instance and prepares it for I/O operations.
      Parameters:
      databaseConnection - the DatabaseConnection instance that provides the JDBC connection to interact with a database.
      fileDescriptor - the FileDescriptor that represents the file to be manipulated in the sequential file operations.

    • EntrySequentialFile

      public EntrySequentialFile(Context context, FileDescriptor fileDescriptor)
      Constructs an EntrySequentialFile object with the specified Context and FileDescriptor. Initializes the instance and prepares it for I/O operations.
      Parameters:
      context - the Context instance used for configuring and managing this sequential file
      fileDescriptor - the FileDescriptor that represents the file to be manipulated in the sequential file operations

    • EntrySequentialFile

      public EntrySequentialFile(Context context, String ddName)
      Constructs an EntrySequentialFile object with the specified context and data definition name. Initializes the instance for sequential file operations.
      Parameters:
      context - the Context instance used for configuring and managing this sequential file
      ddName - the data definition name associated with this sequential file

  • Method Details

    • exceptionsEnabled

      public EntrySequentialFile exceptionsEnabled()
      Enables exception handling for the current EntrySequentialFile instance. This method invokes the superclass implementation to enable exceptions and returns the current instance to allow method chaining.
      Overrides:
      exceptionsEnabled in class AbstractFile
      Returns:
      the current instance of EntrySequentialFile with exceptions enabled

      Returns:
      the current instance of EntrySequentialFile, allowing for method chaining

      recordIs in class AbstractFile
      Parameters:

      recordLength - the length of the record of type NInteger to be associated with this EntrySequentialFile
      Returns:
      the current instance of EntrySequentialFile, allowing for method chaining

    • recordContains

      public EntrySequentialFile recordContains(int min, int max)
      Specifies that records should contain a number of bytes within the specified range. This method invokes the superclass implementation to set the range for the record, and then returns the current instance of EntrySequentialFile to allow method chaining.
      Overrides:
      recordContains in class AbstractFile
      Parameters:
      min - the minimum number of bytes in the record
      max - the maximum number of bytes in the record
      Returns:
      the current instance of EntrySequentialFile, allowing for method chaining

      public EntrySequentialFile relativeKeyIs(NInteger relativeKey)
      Sets the relative key for the current EntrySequentialFile instance.
      Overrides:
      relativeKeyIs in class AbstractEntrySequentialFile
      Parameters:
      relativeKey - the new relative key of type NInteger to be associated with this file
      Returns:
      the current instance of EntrySequentialFile, allowing for method chaining

      Parameters:
      fileStatus - the new file status of type NChar to be associated with this file
      Returns:

    • cached

      public EntrySequentialFile cached()
      Marks the current instance as cached. T
      Overrides:
      cached in class AbstractVsamFile
      Returns:
      the current instance of EntrySequentialFile with caching enabled

    • initIoApi

      public void initIoApi()
      Initializes the IO API for the EntrySequentialFile instance. This method builds the VSAM API using the current instance and the associated database connection, setting up the necessary components for subsequent file operations.
      Specified by:
      initIoApi in class AbstractVsamFile

    • open

      public FileIOResponse open(OpenMode openMode, LockMode lockMode)
      Opens the file with the specified mode and lock type for I/O operations. Depending on the provided open mode, the method initializes internal buffers, sets the appropriate read or write positions, and prepares the file for subsequent I/O operations.
      Overrides:
      open in class AbstractEntrySequentialFile
      Parameters:
      openMode - the mode in which the file should be opened, such as Input, InputOutput, or Append
      lockMode - the locking mode to be applied when accessing the file, such as Share or Exclusive
      Returns:
      an instance of FileIOResponse representing the result of the open operation, including the status and any relevant context

    • read

      public FileIOResponse read(Rba rba, boolean equal, boolean update)
      Reads a record from the file based on the specified RBA and updates the record length accordingly.
      Specified by:
      read in class AbstractEntrySequentialFile
      Parameters:
      rba - the Relative Buffer Address (RBA) used to locate the record within the sequential file
      equal - a boolean flag indicating whether to read a record that matches exactly (true) or to read the next record greater than the specified key (false)
      update - a boolean flag indicating whether the record should allow updates; true to enable update mode, false otherwise
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the operation,

    • start

      public FileIOResponse start(Rba rba, KeyIs mode)
      Starts browsing a record from the file based on the specified RBA and mode.
      Specified by:
      start in class AbstractEntrySequentialFile
      Parameters:
      rba - the Rba key to start from
      mode - the mode in which the file operation should be performed
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the operation,

    • readSequential

      public FileIOResponse readSequential(KeyIs direction, boolean update)
      Reads the next record in the file based on the specified direction and update mode.
      Specified by:
      readSequential in class AbstractVsamFile
      Parameters:
      direction - the direction in which records are to be read, represented by the KeyIs object
      update - a boolean indicating whether the current operation should prepare for updates
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the read operation,

    • write

      public FileIOResponse write()
      Writes the current record from the buffer to the file and handles any errors
      Specified by:
      write in class AbstractVsamFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the operation,

    • rewrite

      public FileIOResponse rewrite()
      Rewrites the current record from the buffer to the file and handles any errors
      Specified by:
      rewrite in class AbstractVsamFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the operation,\

    • close

      public FileIOResponse close()
      Closes the file and releases any resources associated with it.
      Overrides:
      close in class AbstractVsamFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object indicating the result of the close operation.

    • delete

      public FileIOResponse delete()
      For ESDS/RBA files, this operation is not available and will throw a NotImplementedException.
      Specified by:
      delete in class AbstractVsamFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object indicating the result of the delete operation
      Throws:
      NotImplementedException - if the delete operation is not supported
